cases.desc_en: Roulette hits black ten times; a gambler 'knows red is due' and bets big. The eleventh spin is still 50/50 — the past ten are irrelevant. This fallacy fattens casino profits.

cases.desc_zh: 股票連跌七天，投資人認為「總該反彈」而加碼攤平。但每天漲跌是獨立條件機率，連跌不保證明天漲。把賭徒謬誤套用股市，常越攤越深。
cases.desc_en: A stock falls seven days; an investor insists 'a rebound is due' and averages down. But daily moves are independent conditional probabilities — seven reds don't guarantee green. The gambler's fallacy applied to stocks digs the hole deeper.

cases.desc_zh: 連生三個女兒後，有人覺得「第四個總該是兒子」。其實每次懷孕生男機率仍約一半，前三胎不影響第四胎。賭徒謬誤在生活無處不在。
cases.desc_en: After three daughters, someone feels 'the fourth must be a son'. But each pregnancy is still ~50% boy — the first three don't affect the fourth. The gambler's fallacy is everywhere in life.

models.definition_zh: 分組看與合併看，趨勢可能完全相反：A 在每個子群都勝過 B，合併後 A 卻輸給 B。原因往往是各子群樣本比例不均。看數據必須留意分組結構。
models.definition_en: Grouped vs combined views can show opposite trends: A beats B in every subgroup yet loses overall. The cause is often uneven subgroup sample proportions. Reading data demands attention to grouping structure.
models.scenarios_zh: 醫療比較、教育評比、招聘分析。
models.scenarios_en: Medical comparisons, education rankings, hiring analytics.

cases.desc_zh: 療法 A 在大結石與小結石兩組的治癒率都高於 B，但合併所有病人後 B 反而較高。原因是 A 多用於難治的大結石、B 多用於易治的小結石，組成不均造成逆轉。
cases.desc_en: Treatment A beats B for both large and small stones, yet combined B wins — because A was used more on hard large stones and B on easy small ones. Uneven composition reverses the trend.

cases.desc_zh: 某校各系所男性錄取率都不低於女性，全校合計男性錄取率卻較高，看似性別歧視。細看：男性多申請錄取率高的工科、女性多申請競爭激烈的文科，組成造成落差。
cases.desc_en: In every department men's admission rate is no lower than women's, yet men win overall — seeming sexist. In fact men applied more to high-rate engineering and women to competitive humanities; composition explains the gap.

cases.desc_zh: 新版廣告在行動與桌機兩端點擊率都勝過舊版，合併後卻較低。原因是新版曝光集中在低點擊的桌機、舊版集中於高點擊的行動。辛普森悖論提醒別只看合計數字。
cases.desc_en: The new ad beats the old on both mobile and desktop click rates, yet loses overall — because the new ad's impressions concentrated on low-click desktop and the old on high-click mobile. The paradox warns against reading only totals.

models.definition_zh: 物體靜者恆靜、動者恆動，除非受到外力。類推到組織與習慣：現狀有自我延續的力量，啟動改變與停止改變都需要付出額外力量。
models.definition_en: An object at rest stays at rest, in motion stays in motion, unless acted upon. By analogy to organizations and habits: the status quo self-perpetuates; starting and stopping change both require extra force.
models.scenarios_zh: 組織變革、習慣養成、產品轉型。
models.scenarios_en: Organizational change, habit formation, product pivots.

cases.desc_zh: 最難的是前兩週——把靜止的身體啟動要克服慣性。一旦連續運動成為「動者恆動」的狀態，維持反而省力。理解慣性讓你知道撐過啟動期就輕鬆了。
cases.desc_en: The first two weeks are hardest — overcoming inertia to start a body at rest. Once regular exercise becomes 'a body in motion', maintaining it is easier. Understanding inertia means pushing through the start pays off.

cases.desc_zh: 老牌企業的流程、文化、人際網絡都在「動者恆動」，要改方向需極大外力（危機或新領導）。這就是為何改革常要等到存亡之際才動得起來。
cases.desc_en: A legacy firm's processes, culture, and networks are 'in motion'; changing direction needs huge force (crisis or new leadership). That's why reform often only moves at the brink of survival.

cases.desc_zh: 一個每週開、已無實質用途的會議，會因慣性持續數年——沒人記得為何開、也沒人敢停。停止一個進行中的事物，需要刻意的「煞車力」。
cases.desc_en: A weekly meeting with no real purpose persists for years through inertia — no one recalls why it started or dares end it. Stopping something in motion takes deliberate braking force.

models.definition_zh: 質量乘以速度，描述運動的累積力量。類推上，一旦計畫、產品或團隊獲得速度與規模，就帶著一股難以逆轉的推進力，可善用也可提防。
models.definition_en: Mass times velocity — the accumulated force of motion. By analogy, once a project, product, or team gains speed and scale it carries a hard-to-reverse driving force — to be harnessed or guarded against.
models.scenarios_zh: 產品成長、團隊士氣、趨勢判斷。
models.scenarios_en: Product growth, team morale, trend judgment.

cases.desc_zh: 用戶增長→口碑→更多用戶，累積成難以阻擋的動能。早期刻意「加重」（補貼、品質）以建立動能，一旦轉起來，成長會自我加速，對手難以追上。
cases.desc_en: Users grow → word of mouth → more users, accumulating unstoppable momentum. Early on you deliberately 'add mass' (subsidies, quality) to build it; once spinning, growth self-accelerates and rivals can't catch up.

cases.desc_zh: 連勝會累積信心與節奏（正動能），連敗則累積自我懷疑（負動能）。優秀領導者會刻意安排小勝來重建正動能，因為動能比單一戰役更重要。
cases.desc_en: Winning streaks build confidence and rhythm (positive momentum); losing streaks build self-doubt (negative). Good leaders deliberately arrange small wins to rebuild positive momentum — it matters more than any single battle.

cases.desc_zh: 短期內強勢股常繼續強勢、弱勢股繼續弱勢，因資金與心理的動能自我延續。動能投資策略即善用此現象，但也須提防動能反轉時的急殺。
cases.desc_en: In the short run strong stocks tend to keep strong and weak keep weak, as capital and psychological momentum self-perpetuate. Momentum strategies exploit this — but beware sharp reversals.

models.definition_zh: 以小的施力點配合長力臂，移動巨大的重物。類推上，找到高槓桿點——投入小卻影響大——能讓有限資源產生數倍效果。關鍵在找對施力點。
models.definition_en: A small force over a long arm moves a huge load. By analogy, finding a high-leverage point — small input, large impact — lets limited resources yield multiples of effect. The key is the right point to push.
models.scenarios_zh: 管理決策、行銷、個人成長。
models.scenarios_en: Management decisions, marketing, personal growth.

cases.desc_zh: 工廠改善最慢的瓶頸工序一小時，等於改善全線產出數小時。瓶頸就是高槓桿點：在此投入一分力，全線放大數倍。找對施力點勝過全面優化。
cases.desc_en: Improving the slowest bottleneck by one hour equals hours of whole-line output. The bottleneck is the high-leverage point: a unit of effort here multiplies across the line. Pushing the right point beats optimizing everywhere.

cases.desc_zh: 寫一篇深度好文可被轉發數年、帶來持續流量，相較於一次性廣告曝光，是高槓桿的行銷。把時間投入能「重複產出」的資產，槓桿最大。
cases.desc_en: A deep article can be reshared for years, driving continuous traffic — versus one-off ad impressions, it's high-leverage marketing. Time invested in assets that 'keep producing' has the greatest leverage.

cases.desc_zh: 學會「如何學習」這個後設技能，會放大往後所有學習的效率——一次投入，終身受益。投資於能跨領域遷移的底層能力，是人生最高槓桿。
cases.desc_en: Learning 'how to learn' — a meta-skill — multiplies the efficiency of all future learning: invest once, benefit for life. Investing in transferable底层 abilities is life's highest leverage.

models.definition_zh: 阻礙運動的力。類推上，流程中的手續、等待、複雜步驟就是摩擦力——它讓使用者卻步、讓員工浪費精力。降低摩擦常比增加動力更有效。
models.definition_en: The force that resists motion. By analogy, procedures, waits, and complex steps in a flow are friction — they deter users and waste employees' energy. Reducing friction is often more effective than adding driving force.
models.scenarios_zh: 產品設計、流程改善、習慣養成。
models.scenarios_en: Product design, process improvement, habit formation.

cases.desc_zh: 每多一個結帳欄位、多一次頁面跳轉，轉換率就下降。與其加大折扣（增加動力），不如減少必填欄位、支援一鍵結帳（降低摩擦），後者往往效果更好。
cases.desc_en: Each extra checkout field or page jump lowers conversion. Rather than bigger discounts (more driving force), cut required fields and enable one-click checkout (less friction) — often more effective.

cases.desc_zh: 去健身房要通勤、找教練、換裝，這些摩擦讓人放棄。把啞鈴放床邊、運動服擺枕頭旁，把摩擦降到零，習慣自然養成。減摩擦勝過逼意志力。
cases.desc_en: Commuting, finding a trainer, changing — these frictions make people quit. Put dumbbells by the bed and workout clothes by the pillow; zero friction and the habit forms. Reducing friction beats brute willpower.

cases.desc_zh: 報銷要五個章、採購要三級簽核，摩擦讓員工精力耗在流程而非做事。優秀組織會定期「拆摩擦」：簡化簽核、下放權限，釋放執行力。
cases.desc_en: Reimbursements need five stamps, procurement three-level approval — friction drains energy into process instead of work. Great organizations periodically 'remove friction': simplify approvals, delegate authority, unleash execution.

models.definition_zh: 系統會自然趨向混亂、無序，這是熱力學第二定律。房間會自動變亂、程式碼會自動腐化、組織會自動鬆散——維持秩序需持續輸入能量。
models.definition_en: Systems naturally tend toward disorder — the second law of thermodynamics. Rooms get messy, code rots, organizations drift slack: maintaining order requires continuous energy input.
models.scenarios_zh: 系統維護、組織管理、知識整理。
models.scenarios_en: System maintenance, organizational management, knowledge curation.

cases.desc_zh: 沒有持續重構的程式碼會逐漸混亂、耦合加深、難以維護——這是軟體的熵增。定期重構（輸入能量）是抵抗熵增、維持可維護性的唯一方法。
cases.desc_en: Code without continuous refactoring gradually grows tangled and hard to maintain — software's entropy increase. Regular refactoring (energy input) is the only way to resist entropy and keep maintainability.

cases.desc_zh: 房間使用後自動趨向混亂（熵增），要保持整潔必須持續「輸入能量」（收拾）。建立「物歸原位」的日常習慣，是用最小能量對抗熵增的方式。
cases.desc_en: A room tends toward messiness in use (entropy); staying tidy requires continuous 'energy input' (tidying). A daily 'put it back' habit fights entropy with the least energy.

cases.desc_zh: 沒有定期對齊目標與流程的團隊，會自動各自為政、效率下滑——組織的熵增。定期會議、制度梳理、文化宣導，是注入秩序能量的必要動作。
cases.desc_en: A team without regular goal and process alignment drifts into silos and lower efficiency — organizational entropy. Regular meetings, process grooming, and culture reinforcement are the necessary energy of order.

models.definition_zh: 環境會篩選掉不適應的變異、留下適應的，使族群逐代偏向有利特徵。類推上，市場與環境也在不斷「天擇」產品、公司、想法，適者生存。
models.definition_en: The environment filters out maladaptive variants and keeps adaptive ones, shifting a population toward helpful traits over generations. By analogy, markets and environments continually 'select' products, firms, and ideas — survival of the fittest.
models.scenarios_zh: 產品迭代、創業存活、組織演化。
models.scenarios_en: Product iteration, startup survival, organizational evolution.

cases.desc_zh: 推出多個變異版本（A/B 測試），讓市場篩選——轉換率高的留下、低的淘汰。累積數代後，產品「演化」成貼合需求的樣貌，正是商業版天擇。
cases.desc_en: Launch many variant versions (A/B tests) and let the market filter — high-conversion variants survive, low ones die. Over generations the product 'evolves' to fit demand — commercial natural selection.

cases.desc_zh: 市場環境（資金、需求、競爭）如嚴苛大自然，九成新創被淘汰。存活者不是最聰明的，而是最適應當下環境的。理解天擇，創業者應專注「適應」而非「完美」。
cases.desc_en: The market (capital, demand, competition) is a harsh nature that eliminates 90% of startups. Survivors aren't the smartest but the best adapted. Understanding selection, founders should focus on 'fit' not 'perfection'.

cases.desc_zh: 企業內部會自然產生多種做事方式，有效的被模仿留存、無效的被淘汰。久而久之形成「公司文化」。領導者可塑造環境來天擇出想要的文化特徵。
cases.desc_en: Within a firm, multiple ways of working arise; effective ones get copied and survive, ineffective ones die. Over time this becomes 'company culture'. Leaders can shape the environment to select for desired traits.

models.definition_zh: 透過變異、選擇、遺傳的循環，族群逐代累積適應特徵。類推上，任何系統透過「嘗試→篩選→保留→再嘗試」的循環，都能朝更適應環境的方向演化。
models.definition_en: Through cycles of variation, selection, and inheritance, populations accumulate adaptive traits over generations. By analogy, any system cycling 'try → filter → retain → retry' can evolve toward better environmental fit.
models.scenarios_zh: 產品策略、個人成長、知識累積。
models.scenarios_en: Product strategy, personal growth, knowledge accumulation.

cases.desc_zh: 做最小產品（變異）→市場驗證（選擇）→留下有效功能（遺傳）→再迭代。這個演化循環讓產品逐版逼近市場需求，勝過一次性大爆炸式開發。
cases.desc_en: Build a minimum product (variation) → market validates (selection) → keep working features (inheritance) → iterate. This evolutionary cycle approaches fit better than one big-bang build.

cases.desc_zh: 嘗試多種技能（變異）→投入市場測試回饋（選擇）→精進有回報的（遺傳）。多年累積，你的能力組合會演化成獨特且適應市場的樣貌。
cases.desc_en: Try many skills (variation) → test market feedback (selection) → hone the rewarded ones (inheritance). Over years your skill set evolves into a unique, market-adapted profile.

cases.desc_zh: 閱讀多種觀點（變異）→以實踐檢驗（選擇）→納入有效的心智模型（遺傳）。思想體系會像物種般演化，越來越能解釋與應對真實世界。
cases.desc_en: Read diverse views (variation) → test in practice (selection) → adopt working mental models (inheritance). A thought system evolves like a species, increasingly able to explain and navigate the real world.

models.definition_zh: 物種與環境相互依存、循環資源、共同演化而成的整體。類推上，產品、平台、使用者、供應商也構成生態系，單看任何一方都無法理解全貌。
models.definition_en: Species and environment interdepend, cycle resources, and co-evolve into a whole. By analogy, products, platforms, users, and suppliers form ecosystems — no single party can be understood in isolation.
models.scenarios_zh: 平台策略、產業分析、合作網絡。
models.scenarios_en: Platform strategy, industry analysis, partnership networks.

cases.desc_zh: 手機系統的價值不在作業系統本身，而在圍繞它的 App 開發者、使用者、周邊廠商構成的生態系。打敗一個平台往往要打敗整個生態，而非單一產品。
cases.desc_en: A phone OS's value isn't the OS itself but the ecosystem of app developers, users, and accessory makers around it. Beating a platform usually means beating the whole ecosystem, not one product.

cases.desc_zh: 一家車廠的競爭力，依賴周圍零組件商、模具廠、人才學校構成的產業聚落。把供應鏈當生態系經營——共生共榮——比壓榨單點成本更永續。
cases.desc_en: A carmaker's competitiveness depends on the cluster of parts makers, tooling shops, and talent schools around it. Treating the supply chain as an ecosystem — mutual prosperity — beats squeezing single-point costs.

cases.desc_zh: 你的職涯不是單一技能，而是技能、人脈、口碑、作品相互滋養的生態系。經營多樣且互補的資產，比孤注一擲單一技能更能抵抗環境變動。
cases.desc_en: Your career isn't one skill but an ecosystem of skills, network, reputation, and work that feed one another. Cultivating diverse, complementary assets resists environmental shocks better than betting on a single skill.

models.definition_zh: 收益再投入產生收益，使成長呈指數而非線性。複利的力量在「時間」與「不中斷」：小小的持續投入，經數十年能累積成驚人成果。
models.definition_en: Returns reinvested generate their own returns, making growth exponential rather than linear. Compounding's power is 'time' and 'no interruption': small sustained inputs compound into astonishing results over decades.
models.scenarios_zh: 投資、學習、人脈、健康。
models.scenarios_en: Investing, learning, networks, health.

cases.desc_zh: 每年 8% 報酬，一萬本金 30 年後變十萬——其中九成來自複利而非本金。複利說明：時間是普通人最強的財富槓桿，越早開始越佔便宜。
cases.desc_en: At 8% a year, 10,000 becomes 100,000 in 30 years — 90% from compounding, not principal. Compounding shows time is the ordinary person's strongest wealth lever; starting early wins.

cases.desc_zh: 每學一個新觀念，都會與既有知識產生連結、衍生新理解。讀得越多，吸收新知越快——這是知識的複利。每天讀半小時，十年後差距巨大。
cases.desc_en: Each new idea links to existing knowledge and spawns new understanding. The more you read, the faster you absorb — knowledge compounding. Half an hour daily makes a huge gap in ten years.

cases.desc_zh: 幫過的人介紹新朋友、新朋友又帶來機會，關係網呈指數擴張。早期刻意付出、不計回報地建立信任，多年後人脈會以複利方式回報你。
cases.desc_en: People you help introduce new friends, who bring opportunities — your network expands exponentially. Deliberately giving and building trust early pays back in compounded ways years later.
